sanskrit:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः।
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते॥
transliteration:
satataṃ kīrtayanto māṃ yatantaś ca dṛḍha-vratāḥ |
namasyantaś ca māṃ bhaktyā nitya-yuktā upāsate ||
literal_meaning: Always glorifying Me, striving with firm vows, bowing to Me in devotion, perpetually united they worship Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the psychological profile of the ‘great-souled’: a mind locked in an unbroken flow of appreciative attention, sustained by disciplined effort and reverence, producing a stable state of absorbed worship.
psychological_state: Secure obsessive devotion—cognitive resources are channeled into a single, meaningful object, creating flow, ego-transcendence and resilient well-being.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out professional replaces doom-scrolling with nightly 10-minute gratitude journaling about a person/ideal they revere; within weeks rumination drops, energy returns, and life feels coherent again.
anchor_text: Keep your mind steadily fixed on Me with love, effort, and reverent awe.
